channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: New Zealand moves to criminalise non-consensual sexual deepfakes and considers regulating apps
item.description: New Zealand is set to introduce the Deepfake Digital Harm and Exploitation Bill, which will criminalise the creation, distribution, or sale of sexualised deepfakes without consent. The move responds t...

item.title: DPWH officials removed after cyclist dies from road collapse in Benguet
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Two officials from the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) in the Cordillera region, Isagani Cayme and Marjory Banao, have been relieved of their duties following the ...

item.title: How Sedentary Lifestyle Affects Men's Health
item.description: The article dey show wetin fit happen if man just siddon down too much and no dey move. E start with story of Chris, 47, wey dey struggle small to even stand from sofa because him body don dey weak fr...

item.title: Trump Signals Potential Military Action Against Iran Amid Nuclear Dispute
item.description: US President Donald Trump has revealed that a planned military strike on Iran was postponed at the last minute, claiming the attack was only 'an hour away'. He stated that US warships are fully prepar...

item.title: Ambira Boys High School Closed Indefinitely Following Student Unrest Over Exam Cheating Claims
item.description: Ambira Boys High School in Siaya County was closed indefinitely after students staged a violent রাত rampage on the night of May 18, causing widespread destruction of school property worth millions of ...

item.title: Arsenal secure first Premier League title in 22 years following Man City draw
item.description: Arsenal have been crowned Premier League champions for the first time in 22 years, ending a long wait since their Invincibles season in 2004. Mikel Arteta's side had been runners-up in the previous th...

item.title: Google Launches Pics, an AI Design Tool for Workspace Users
item.description: At its 2026 Google I/O event, Google introduced Pics, a new AI-powered design and image-generation tool integrated into Google Workspace. Pics aims to make visual creation accessible to a broad range ...

item.title: Medical Examiner Rules Death of 12-Year-Old California Girl Due to Natural Causes Despite Head Injury
item.description: Khimberly Zavaleta Chuquipa, a 12-year-old student at Reseda Charter High School, died days after being hit in the head by a metal water bottle during an alleged bullying incident. Despite initial rep...

item.title: Centre assures steady fertiliser supply to Punjab farmers despite global price rise
item.description: Punjab BJP president Sunil Jakhar on 19th May 2026 stated that despite the ongoing Gulf conflict causing a global surge in fertiliser prices, especially urea and di-ammonium phosphate (DAP), the Union...

item.title: Matatu Operators Reject Diesel Price Deal, Strike to Continue
item.description: Nairobi — The Kenyan government faced a public embarrassment after matatu operators rejected an announcement by Energy CS Opiyo Wandayi, claiming a deal had been reached on fuel prices. During a media...

item.title: Arsenal clinch Premier League title after 22 years with fans celebrating in North London
item.description: Arsenal have ended a 22-year wait for a Premier League title, following Manchester City's draw against Bournemouth in Pep Guardiola's penultimate game in charge. The Gunners, managed by Mikel Arteta, ...

item.title: Thomas Massie Loses Republican Primary to Trump-Endorsed Challenger
item.description: In a significant political development in Kentucky, Representative Thomas Massie (R-KY), known for his independent streak and opposition to certain GOP positions, lost his re-nomination to Ed Gallrein...

item.title: Trump Issues Warning of Possible Military Action Against Iran Amid Stalled Negotiations
item.description: On May 19, 2026, former President Donald Trump renewed his threat to initiate military action against Iran if the country does not meet U.S. demands in ongoing negotiations. Speaking to reporters, Tru...

item.title: Tanzania assures citizens of fair benefits in US health cooperation talks
item.description: Tanzania has promised to safeguard national interests as it negotiates a new health cooperation agreement with the United States. Health Minister Mohamed Mchengerwa said the talks are designed to avoi...

item.title: Democrats aim to gain two seats in Georgia Supreme Court elections
item.description: In the upcoming Georgia Supreme Court elections, Democrats are making a concerted effort to gain two seats on the state's nonpartisan court. The party is supporting personal injury attorney Miracle Ra...

item.title: Health Risks of Manicures and Pedicures: How to Stay Safe
item.description: Manicures and pedicures are hugely popular, with the global nail care market valued at around US$23.5 billion. However, visiting a nail salon carries some risk of infection, especially if hygiene stan...

item.title: Calcutta HC directs CBI to submit all RG Kar case investigation materials
item.description: The Calcutta High Court on Tuesday directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to submit all documents, audio recordings, videos, and photographs related to the RG Kar rape-murder case. The cou...

item.title: Multiple fires in Riverside County injure five and prompt evacuations
item.description: On May 19, 2026, multiple brush fires erupted in Riverside County, California, amid dry, warm conditions and gusty winds. The largest, the Bain fire, started in the Jurupa Valley near the Santa Ana Ri...

item.title: Ludhiana Punjabi singer Yashinder Kaur murdered after abduction, body recovered from canal
item.description: In Ludhiana, a tragic incident unfolded as Punjabi singer Yashinder Kaur, also known as Inder Kaur, aged 29, was found murdered six days after her alleged abduction at gunpoint. Police investigations ...

item.title: Yoko Ono’s Pioneering Artistic Work and Public Life in the 1970s
item.description: Yoko Ono, born February 18, 1933, is a Japanese artist, musician, activist, and filmmaker whose groundbreaking work in the 1970s solidified her as a key figure in avant-garde art. During this decade, ...
